title: MAST — Materials Inverse Design (RL)
blurb: An inverse-design engine: enter the mechanical properties you want, and a reinforcement-learning agent proposes the steel composition and heat-treatment route to achieve them.
problem: Designing a steel to hit a target set of mechanical properties is normally trial-and-error across a huge composition + heat-treatment space — slow, expensive, and dependent on metallurgist intuition.
solution: Built a policy-gradient RL agent (PPO) that searches over composition and heat-treatment actions, rewarded by a feed-forward MLP ensemble 'forward model' that predicts resulting properties with uncertainty. Benchmarked against a KNN baseline and served through an interactive Streamlit app.
impact: Turns property targets directly into ranked, actionable process recipes (austenitize → quench → temper) with uncertainty estimates — the core loop of autonomous experiment planning. Deployed and publicly usable.
tech: Python, PyTorch, PPO / Policy Gradients, Scikit-learn, Streamlit

title: MAST — Steel Property Prediction
blurb: A composition-to-property prediction pipeline that estimates the mechanical properties of steel from its chemistry and processing — the basis of my B.Eng. thesis (90%+ accuracy).
problem: Measuring mechanical properties (tensile, hardness, fatigue) experimentally for every candidate steel is costly and slow, bottlenecking materials screening.
solution: End-to-end ML pipeline from raw iron-carbon phase-diagram and composition data through feature engineering, model training (XGBoost), and SHAP explainability — exposing which features drive each prediction. Deployed as a live Streamlit tool.
impact: Achieved 90%+ prediction accuracy on held-out data and made the model interpretable via SHAP, so predictions are trustworthy rather than black-box. Awarded the 2024 Computational Materials Engineer Class Award.
tech: Python, XGBoost, Scikit-learn, SHAP, Pandas, Streamlit

title: MAST — Galvanic Corrosion PINN
blurb: A physics-informed graph neural network that predicts galvanic corrosion behaviour in multi-material assemblies, with the electrochemistry enforced inside the loss function.
problem: Galvanic corrosion between dissimilar metals is hard to predict from data alone — purely statistical models can produce physically impossible results on unseen material pairs.
solution: Built a graph PINN (PyTorch Geometric) treating materials as nodes and joints as edges, with Kirchhoff's Current Law and thermodynamic-consistency penalties baked into the loss. Predicts galvanic current density and material compatibility, served via a Streamlit dashboard with a full galvanic-series reference.
impact: Produces physically plausible corrosion predictions even for material pairs never seen in training — useful for real engineering material-selection decisions. Deployed and publicly usable.
tech: Python, PyTorch, PyTorch Geometric, NNConv / Graph NN, Streamlit

title: CFD-ML — Simulation Surrogate / Digital Twin
blurb: A neural-network surrogate that replaces expensive CFD solver runs — a working digital twin achieving roughly 10x speedup while keeping engineering-grade accuracy.
problem: Iterative design needs hundreds of CFD evaluations, but each high-fidelity COMSOL / ANSYS Fluent run is computationally expensive, making design sweeps painfully slow.
solution: Built an automated pipeline that runs parametric CFD sweeps (PyFluent), harvests and structures the simulation outputs, and trains a neural surrogate mapping geometry + boundary conditions to steady-state flow fields in milliseconds.
impact: ~10x faster than running the physics solver, enabling rapid design iteration and demonstrating that ML can stand in for expensive physics runs inside materials/engineering design loops.
tech: Python, TensorFlow, ANSYS Fluent (PyFluent), COMSOL, NumPy

title: OPsonic — Acoustic Threat Detection
blurb: An audio-security intelligence system that classifies threat sounds — gunshots, glass breaking, sirens — from raw audio, built for DataVessel Lab.
problem: Physical-security monitoring needs to recognise dangerous acoustic events in real time, reliably, across noisy real-world environments.
solution: Designed a full ML pipeline (data → spectrogram features → model → REST microservice). Trained an Audio Spectrogram Transformer on UrbanSound8K + ESC-50 security subsets with fold-based validation to prevent leakage, containerised with Docker and deployed as a production inference API.
impact: ~99% top-1 (99.9% top-3) accuracy with near-perfect recall on critical classes like gunshot and glass-breaking; remaining errors are intuitive acoustic confusions. Shipped as a deployable microservice.
tech: Python, PyTorch, Audio Spectrogram Transformer, CNN+LSTM, Docker, REST API

title: FajrGuard — On-Device AI Prayer Guardian
blurb: A mobile app whose escalating alarm can only be dismissed by performing wudu, verified on-device by a custom computer-vision model in under 100ms.
problem: Standard alarms are trivially snoozed. The goal was an alarm that genuinely gets a user up and to prayer — requiring a real-world action it can verify, fully offline and privately.
solution: Built a React Native (Expo) app with a custom CNN trained from scratch in PyTorch, quantised and bundled for on-device TFLite inference — detecting face moisture (wudu) plus identity verification, with critical-alert alarms and background tasks.
impact: Sub-100ms on-device inference with no data leaving the phone; demonstrates rapid prototyping from problem definition all the way to a working, privacy-preserving mobile AI product.
tech: React Native (Expo), PyTorch, TensorFlow Lite, FastAPI, Vision Camera

title: Iqra-Books — E-Book Reader (PWA + Mobile)
blurb: An Apple Books-inspired e-reader delivered as an installable PWA and as native iOS/Android apps from a single codebase.
problem: Readers want a clean, fast, offline-capable reading experience across web and mobile without maintaining three separate apps.
solution: Built a React + Vite reader with a serverless backend (Netlify Functions) for search and content proxying, wrapped with Capacitor to ship native iOS/Android builds, and made fully installable/offline as a PWA.
impact: One codebase → web, installable PWA, and native mobile apps; a clean, polished reading UX with offline support.
tech: React, Vite, Capacitor, Netlify Functions, Service Workers (PWA)

title: Enterprise Management System
blurb: A cloud-native enterprise management platform with automated ML-powered data pipelines, deployed on Kubernetes and serving 100+ users.
problem: An organisation needed a scalable internal platform that combined day-to-day management with automated, ML-driven data processing — without fragile manual workflows.
solution: Architected and deployed a FastAPI + React.js system on Kubernetes with containerised ML microservices, CI/CD, monitoring, and REST inference endpoints — a full MLOps lifecycle from experiment to production.
impact: Production cloud-native infrastructure serving 100+ users with automated ML pipelines, zero-downtime deployments, and observability built in.
tech: FastAPI, React.js, Docker, Kubernetes, MongoDB, CI/CD, Nginx

title: Advanced Pentesting & Automation
blurb: Custom automation tooling and deep-dive API security assessments that find the business-logic flaws automated scanners miss.
problem: Routine penetration tests are time-consuming and prone to missing complex, chained vulnerabilities; standard tools lack context for specific API business logic.
solution: Developed a suite of custom recon and scanning automation (Python, Bash) to widen coverage, and ran deep manual API assessments to identify and exploit business-logic flaws (IDOR, broken access control).
impact: Cut manual testing time by ~40% and surfaced several critical API vulnerabilities that automated scanners missed, preventing potential data breaches.
tech: Python, Bash, Burp Suite, Postman, API Security

title: VTOL UAV — Design & Composite Simulation
blurb: A custom hybrid VTOL fixed-wing UAV designed in SolidWorks, fabricated in carbon-fiber composite, and validated with FEA and CFD — flown as a real prototype.
problem: Standard UAV frames were too heavy and failed under high-stress, long-endurance flight profiles, and needed VTOL capability without sacrificing forward-flight efficiency.
solution: Designed a quad-lift + fixed-wing airframe in SolidWorks, ran FEA (ANSYS) and CFD flow simulation to optimise aerodynamics and structure, and fabricated it from lightweight carbon-fiber composite. Explore the 3D models and CFD animations in the gallery.
impact: A lighter airframe with substantially higher structural integrity under simulated flight loads — built and flown as a working prototype.
tech: SolidWorks, ANSYS (FEA, CFD), Carbon Fiber, 3D Printing

title: SiC-Lead Composite Armour — Ballistic FEA
blurb: High-velocity impact simulation of a silicon-carbide / lead composite armour, characterising stress-wave propagation across a parameter sweep.
problem: Armour design needs to understand how a ballistic impact's stress waves propagate and dissipate through layered composite materials before committing to physical testing.
solution: Set up and ran ANSYS Explicit Dynamics simulations of SiC-lead composite armour under high-velocity impact, sweeping parameters and analysing stress-wave propagation across configurations.
impact: Provided a computational basis for armour material/geometry decisions and demonstrated systematic simulation-study workflow for defence-sector analysis.
tech: ANSYS Explicit Dynamics, Composite Materials, Impact Mechanics

title: Bio-Neuron Chip — Microelectrode Array (UC San Diego)
blurb: Microelectrode-array and microfluidic-channel design for a bio-neuron chip, in collaboration with a UC San Diego Bioengineering doctoral researcher.
problem: Interfacing with living neurons on-chip requires electrode arrays and fluidic channels designed within strict nanoscale fabrication constraints.
solution: Designed microelectrode arrays and microfluidic channels informed by nanoscale fabrication limits, integrating domain science (bioengineering) with manufacturable engineering design.
impact: Delivered fabrication-ready designs for an international research collaboration, bridging bioengineering requirements and materials/manufacturing reality.
